projects.title: Braver Search
projects.description: Apple won't let you change Safari's default search engine outside its limited options. So I built a workaround that feels native and invisible. Now has over **20k daily active users**.

projects.title: Cycles MCP
projects.description: Tools like Speckit add too much overhead — it's easy to invest time speccing around incorrect assumptions with no room to course correct. Cycles is a lightweight alternative that turns product requirements into right-sized dev tasks and handles git automation.

projects.title: Jumblebook Notes
projects.description: When I built this, Apple didn't support password-protected notes. Now I keep it running for the loyal users who've been using it for journaling, book writing, storing passwords, and script writing.

projects.title: Ciderpress
projects.description: Apple's App Store preview video requirements are a guess-and-check game — vague errors, no clear spec. I reverse-engineered the exact formatting requirements and built a tool that handles it using ffmpeg wasm.

projects.title: Riffolio
projects.description: AI lowered the barrier to build, but just because you can doesn't mean you should. So I'm combining blind peer ratings with AI scoring to surface which ideas are actually worth building.

projects.title: Poolside
projects.description: Many NFL picks leagues still run on Excel and email. No app handles the full loop — picking against the spread, tracking live scores, and knowing where you stand in the pool. So I built one.

projects.title: CodeMaverick
projects.description: Technical interviews still feel nothing like how engineers actually build. That seems fixable.

projects.title: BookmarX
projects.description: X bookmarks are a graveyard — great content saved and never revisited because of a poor UX.
